LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF RICCI CORNELL'S MOTION FOR ORDER STAYING ENFORCEMENT OF ORDER APPOINTING RECEIVER IS GRANTED. THE FEBRUARY 4, 2014 ORDER APPOINTING A RECEIVER IS STAYED PENDING APPEAL. CCP SEC. 917.5 IS NOT DISCRETIONARY AND THEREFORE THE ORDER MUST BE STAYED, PROVIDED PLAINTIFF POSTS THE UNDERTAKING. THE TIMELINESS OF PLAINTIFF'S APPEAL IS DETERMINED BY THE APPELLATE COURT. (SEE CAL. RULE OF COURT 8.104, "IF A NOTICE OF APPEAL IS FILED LATE, THE REVIEWING COURT MUST DISMISS THE APPEAL.") THE STAY WILL TAKE EFFECT UPON PLAINTIFF POSTING AN UNDERTAKING IN THE AMOUNT OF $50,000. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records
Off Calendar Mar-24-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Phillip Seltzer of Rubinstein Law telephone number (415) 967-1970 for Plaintiff and Defendants in intervention.
Daniel Iannitelli of Iannitelli Law Office telephone number (415) 324-8801 for Intervenor Ckarisse Lacau.

The Parties stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem. The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, DEFENDANT RICCI CORNELL, NICOLE DEWALD, DECOR BAR, INC.'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena for Production of Business Records is granted without prejudice subject to condition of filing a motion for relief of stay in the Bankruptcy Court. The request for sanctions award is taken under consideration. A proposed order is to be prepared by Counsel for the Intervenor.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Staying Enforcement Of Order Appointing Receiver
Off Calendar Jul-08-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PHILLIP SELTZER OF RUBINSTEIN LAW, TEL. NO. 967-1970 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF RICCI CORNELL'S Motion For Order Staying Enforcement Of Order Appointing Receiver is Granted. The February 4, 2014 Order appointing a receiver is stayed pending appeal. CCP sec. 917.5 is not discretionary and therefore the order must be stayed, provided Plaintiff posts the undertaking. The timeliness of Plaintiff's appeal is determined by the appellate court. (See Cal. Rule of Court 8.104, "If a notice of appeal is filed late, the reviewing court must dismiss the appeal.") The stay will take effect upon Plaintiff posting an undertaking in the amount of $50,000. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/ehg

Order To Show Cause Hearing Why A Preliminary Injunction Should Not Issue
Off Calendar Dec-10-2013 Continued To Jan-28-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MOTION TO INTERVENE
Off Calendar Nov-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: PHILIP SELTZER, ESQ., 415.967.1970, FOR PLAINTIFF RICCI CORNELL.
STEVEN POLLAK, ESQ., 650.583.5500, FOR INTERVENOR CLARICE LACAU.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: INTERVENOR CLARICE LACAU'S MOTION TO INTERVENE IS Granted. The proposed complaint in intervention shall be filed within 10 days of the entry of this order. Intervenor is entitled to this relief pursuant to Corp. Code 1800. Plaintiff's request to strike the corporation's answer is denied as procedurally improper and substantively inappropriate. Both the corporation and intervenor are entitled to assert their positions in opposition to plaintiff's complaint. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
